title: Fostering breakthrough AI innovation through customer-back engineering
optimized_headline: Unlocking AI Innovation: How Customer-Back Engineering Drives Breakthroughs
raw_excerpt: Despite years of digitization, organizations capture less than one-third of the value expected from digital investments, according to McKinsey research. raw_body: Despite years of digitization, organizations capture less than one-third of the value expected from digital investments, according to McKinsey research. That’s because most big companies begin with technological capabilities and bolt applications onto them, rather than starting with customer needs and working backward to technology solutions. Not prioritizing the customer can create fragmented solutions; disjointed…
speedrun: A recent McKinsey study reveals that organizations are only realizing about 30% of the potential value from their digital investments. This shortfall often stems from a focus on technology first, rather than understanding customer needs. By failing to prioritize the customer, companies risk creating fragmented solutions that don’t effectively address real-world problems. This insight is crucial now as businesses seek to maximize their digital strategies amid increasing competition.
why_it_matters: Companies that shift focus to customer needs could see improved satisfaction and loyalty, enhancing their market position.
This approach signals a broader trend in the industry towards customer-centric innovation, potentially reshaping product development strategies.

lenses.eli12: Most companies aren’t getting the full benefits of their digital tools because they focus on technology instead of what customers really want. It’s like building a car without asking people what features they need. By starting with customer needs, businesses could create better solutions that fit real-life situations. This matters to everyday people because it could lead to products and services that truly meet their needs.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this means understanding that starting with customer needs can enhance product relevance. By aligning technology with user expectations, they could reduce wasted resources and improve efficiency. This approach encourages more thoughtful product development, leading to solutions that resonate better with users and ultimately drive growth.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the McKinsey findings suggest that organizations should employ customer-back engineering to optimize their digital investments. This approach could lead to more cohesive and effective solutions, as engineers align their work with user needs rather than just technological capabilities. The challenge lies in integrating customer insights into the development process, which may require new tools or methodologies.
